segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Deep Hall opened not gradually, but all at once -- as if a cathedral of stone had been hiding an arm's length beyond the dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pillars like old oaks rose from an obsidian floor polished to mirror.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Bas-reliefs coiled up each: dwarves and dragons working side by side; forges burning in colors metal shouldn't make; the nine pedestals carved and blessed, each one distinct in its geometry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the crown of every pillar, chains looped around a shape no artist would define -- something that might have been a lock, or a heart, or both.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Along the walls, nine alcoves expressed their schools in stone:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Evocation: facets that multiplied light until it looked like heat, angular and aggressive.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Abjuration: nested bevels, each guarding the next in concentric protection.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Conjuration: empty arches promising somewhere else, frames without destinations.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Divination: spirals your eyes couldn't follow without falling, probability made geometry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Enchantment: curves that made attention lean, seductive and subtle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Illusion: surfaces that adjusted when you didn't look, never quite the same twice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Necromancy: a hollow; an absence; a worn edge where something used to be.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Transmutation: rough stone flowing to glass mid-surface, frozen mid-change.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: - Destruction: edges broken on purpose to prove a point, jagged and defiant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: In the center, a round granite table big enough for a parliament. Dust softened the shine. No footprints but theirs.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The wards held,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik breathed, watching his scanner's needles settle to a calm green.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Active peace. Saints and stone -- this place is safe."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana still paced the perimeter -- old habits dying hard. Her light swept high vaults and alcoves; nothing moved but breath and dust motes dancing in the ward-glow. Vex tested for pressure plates out of reflex, found none. Elowen's voice came out as reverent as prayer.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stone remembers bargains."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan paused at the threshold. The carved runes flickered -- curious, then tolerant.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Uncomfortable,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said honestly, and crossed. His shadow tried to widen past his boots; the ward-light nudged it back like a shepherd dog redirecting wayward sheep. He set his jaw. It obeyed.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We rest,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana decided.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Pairs on watch. No solos."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They burned a small, clean fire in a shallow stone cradle with invisible vents -- Thornik tapped the pillar and grinned:

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We make smoke go where we want. Grandfather's diagrams called this 'breathstone.' Channels run through the walls."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The air tasted faintly of oil and old ash; fresher scents -- leather, metal polish, juniper from a scrap of kindling -- cut through the mineral cold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ate dried meat and hard cheese. They checked steel, adjusted straps, cleaned blades. They didn't talk much at first.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin sat with his back to a pillar beneath Evocation, watching the facets multiply the firelight into something that looked almost like the flames he carried.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale had cooled to its steady coal-glow; the constant ache was back -- the kind Elowen could ease when it wasn't fresh-burn. He flexed his hand. The lattice answered, not offended, not appeased.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: You'll know when you can pay again, it seemed to say.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hoped his body would warn him before his bone-marrow did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: First Watch: Caelin & Vex

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They kept it simple: steel on knees, backs to stone, ears tuned to the empty halls. The scale pulsed with his heart, ember drifting like slow fish under dark glass.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That thing is you now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, not unkind, studying the plate where it lay flush in his skin.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Optional to like it. Not optional to learn it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He huffed a laugh.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It calls me out when I lie. When anyone does."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He nodded at his arm.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "White spark. Like thorns under the skin."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Handy and infuriating."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She studied him with those mismatched eyes.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "For what it's worth -- you didn't hesitate when it acted. Courage or stupidity."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Which do you vote?"

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Yes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She eased a breath around her ribs, wincing slightly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Brave people do stupid things with better reasons."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "And you joke so you don't scream."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't get romantic. Joking also annoys monsters."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed quiet. Truth accepted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They sat in companionable silence after that, listening to the fire pop and the mountain breathe.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Second Watch: Serana & Thornik

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's hands rested in prayer form, fingers laced. No words came. Her lips moved occasionally but made no sound. Thornik pretended to tune his scanner while watching her not-pray.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Still split?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he asked gently after a long silence.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Protect yesterday. Sacrifice today."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mouth crooked, humorless.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I can follow orders. I cannot follow contradictions."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "In a good system, contradictions signal a changed input."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tapped his grandfather's journal.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "He wrote: Balance isn't the absence of cost. It's paying without bankrupting the world."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Doesn't make it easier."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said softly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "But it makes it necessary."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her light flickered gold?silver?gold again; she steadied it with a breath and nodded, accepting the truth if not the comfort of it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They sat watching the shadows dance on ancient carvings, and neither spoke of the weight they carried.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Third Watch: Nyxara & Elowen

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Silence sat between them like a cat that owned the room.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Your magic is... angled,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said at last, breaking the quiet.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "So is yours,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara replied, head tilted with feline curiosity.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Yours bends toward growth and cycles. Mine bends toward... consent, of a kind."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Do you trust it?"

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Trust implies parity."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A small, tired smile.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I honor the bargains I make."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "I'm losing ground,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said softly, touching the edge of the blackened vine-mark with careful fingers.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "When he casts, I can't help. I burn."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Then plan for loss,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said. Not cruel. Precise.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Decide who you are when the Weave doesn't answer."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen looked at the Necromancy alcove without fear, studying the hollow where something had been carved away.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I don't know."

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Decide anyway."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A frost halo settled on Nyxara's lashes for a heartbeat and melted, leaving tiny droplets like tears that weren't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen noticed. Said nothing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Fourth Watch: Durgan (breaking the pair rule)

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He took the arch alone and didn't wake the person assigned to him. Not defiance -- necessity. When he slept, his shadow got ideas.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He kept it small, neat, obedient by force, the way you keep your breathing even under a predator's gaze. The scale's distant glow tugged at his periphery like a road that always slopes downward, whispering promises in a language he refused to learn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Walk away, a voice inside offered. Lose yourself in the dark. Stop being a danger.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't. Instead he squeezed four half-moons into his thigh through wool and felt the pain anchor him. Added four more next to the earlier set, a constellation of control etched in bruises.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His shadow moved anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not far -- an inch, maybe two, stretching toward the coal-glow at the edge of Caelin's bedroll. He caught it the moment he felt the resistance ease, snapped it back with a breath so sharp it hurt his chest, and held it there. It obeyed. Barely.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He made reaching boring. Made it cost. But it had moved on its own, and they both knew it.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "How long,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he thought, not asking anyone,

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "before I can't hold it still?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ward-lines along the threshold pulsed once -- assessing, not judging. They recognized restraint when they saw it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't move.
